newsSince: No material company-specific news since June 9. Australian Retirement Trust lifted its stake to 6.23% (filed June 12), a mild ownership signal. The latest hard catalyst remains the January 2026 US$9.1M US-government aerospace cooling follow-on, already in the score. (Note: a June 1 'Peter Warren Automotive' guidance cut relates to a different ASX:PWR ticker, not PWR Holdings.)

sources.label: Australian Retirement Trust lifts PWR Holdings stake to 6.23% (Kalkine)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material company news; the institutional stake increase is a mild signal, not a thermal-thesis catalyst. The pre-discovery motorsport/aerospace-to-datacenter cooling asymmetry is neither newly validated nor crowded, so the strict-screen #2 / Non-US#1 placement holds.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Repurposing aerospace/motorsport cooling for data-center thermal
verdicts.primaryRisk: Buyer discovery hasn't happened; thesis remains speculative
verdicts.bull: Proven high-performance heat-exchanger IP and defense/aerospace wins could be discovered by data-center thermal buyers before peers.
verdicts.neutral: No catalyst since January; quality micro-cap thermal optionality awaiting validation.
verdicts.bear: Niche motorsport/aerospace revenue base; data-center cooling pivot is unproven and small-cap illiquid.
